revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kabutoyama Kofun (Yasu)]][[ja:甲山古墳 (野洲市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kabutoyama Kofun (Yasu)]][[ja:甲山古墳 (野洲市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kabutoyama Kofun (Yasu)]][[ja:甲山古墳 (野洲市)]] [[ja:甲山古墳 (野洲市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.074617|N|136.04025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}}} '''Kabutoyama Kofun''' (甲山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kosonohara, Yasu City|ja|野洲市小篠原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosonohara, Yasu City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|circular Kofun (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|en|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|qid=Q104904117|lt=Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|1=Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster}}, a National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}. This kofun features one of the largest horizontal stone chambers and stone sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture. == Overview == {{center|[[File:Kabutoyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]}} Kabutoyama Kofun is located on the northern end of the hills extending from {{ill|Ōiwayama|ja|大岩山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiwayama|qq=}}, on the right bank of the {{ill|Yasu_River|en|Yasu_River|qid=Q8049935|lt=Yasu River|1=Yasu River}} in southern Shiga Prefecture. Although the tomb has been plundered, archaeological investigations were conducted as part of site preservation efforts in recent years. The mound is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. No evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} or {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} has been found on the surface. The burial facility is a {{ill|single-entry horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/single-entry horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the west, with a total length of 14.2 meters, making it the largest of its kind in Shiga Prefecture. The chamber contains a hewn {{ill|house-shaped stone sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of volcanic tuff from the Aso region, referred to as "Aso pink stone" or "Mamon stone." It is the largest sarcophagus in the prefecture. Though many grave goods were scattered due to looting, some luxurious artifacts were recovered during excavations, including rare items such as horse armor and gold threads. The construction date of the kofun is estimated to be the mid-6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (some sources suggest the early 6th century). Artifacts from the tomb show connections to the southern {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|qid=Q483134}}, and the grandeur of the tomb’s features and burial goods indicates the prominence of the person interred. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1941. Today, it is part of the Sakura Basa Historical Park (桜生史跡公園), which is open to the public, although access to the burial chamber is restricted except for annual autumn events. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features the following dimensions: * Total length: 14.3 meters * Main chamber (玄室): Length 6.6 meters, width 2.9 meters, height 3.3 meters * Corridor (羨道): Length 7.5 meters, width approximately 1.8 meters The chamber includes a sloping ceiling formed by overlapping stones, with the main chamber floor covered in pebbles and traces of cinnabar and mica. The stone sarcophagus measures 2.6 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height, with decorative projections for securing ropes during transport. Both the interior and exterior of the sarcophagus are coated with red ochre and bengara pigment. The tomb chamber has been looted multiple times, but artifacts such as beads, horse armor, and gold decorations have been recovered. Only two glass beads were found inside the sarcophagus. Kabutoyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (looking towards the rear wall) Kabutoyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-3.jpg|Main chamber (looking towards the entrance) Kabutoyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (looking towards the entrance) Kabutoyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (looking towards the main chamber) == Artifacts == Significant artifacts recovered during investigations include: * Ornaments: ** Gold thread ** Pendants (fish-shaped, petal-shaped, and rhomboid-shaped) ** Gold-plated metal plates * Beads: ** Glass beads (red, yellow, blue, green) ** Rounded beads ** Silver mountain-shaped beads ** Jasper cut beads ** Wooden lacquered beads ** Crystal abacus beads ** Dragonfly beads * Weapons: ** Armor ** Horse armor ** Swords ** Iron spears ** Iron arrowheads ** Gold-plated quiver fittings * Horse gear: ** Saddle fittings ** Harness fittings ** Bell-shaped bit fittings * Tools: ** Iron nails ** Iron axes ** Hook-shaped iron tools Additionally, two {{ill|beast-belt mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beast-belt mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—a type of bronze mirror—were reportedly found in 1898 and are now housed at the {{ill|Kyushu National Museum|en|Kyushu National Museum|qid=Q148543|lt=Kyushu National Museum|1=Kyushu National Museum}}. The mirrors are thought to have been part of Kabutoyama Kofun’s burial goods. 三上山下古墳出土 獣帯鏡-1.JPG|Beast-belt mirror
({{ill|Yasu City History and Folklore Museum|ja|野洲市歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasu City History and Folklore Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) 三上山下古墳出土 獣帯鏡-2.JPG|Beast-belt mirror
({{ill|Yasu City History and Folklore Museum|ja|野洲市歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasu City History and Folklore Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) == Related Facilities == * Sakura Basa Historical Park Information Center (野洲市小篠原) * Yasu City History and Folklore Museum (野洲市辻町) == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Kabutoyama Kofun (Yasu)}} * {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|en|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|qid=Q104904117|lt=Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|1=Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster}} {{translated page|ja|甲山古墳 (野洲市)|version=101959783|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:甲山古墳 (野洲市)]] {{Commons category|Kabutoyama Kofun (Yasu)}} {{wikidata link|Q63386717}} [[Category:Kofun of Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yasu]] [[Category:National historic sites in Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]]
